title: Lugares
goals: ¿Cómo hablo de mi lugar de residencia?
There is/there are, can, shopping phrases, prepositions, places, rooms and furniture.
Describir espacios, comprar y dar direcciones.

aspects.title: La oficina y el museo
aspects.summary: Describir un espacio, coordinar una salida y preguntar horarios.

aspects.title: There is / there are
aspects.summary: Decir qué hay: afirmativo, negativo e interrogativo.

aspects.title: Can: poder y pedir
aspects.summary: Habilidades, lo que se puede hacer en un lugar, y pedidos.

aspects.title: Preposiciones de lugar
aspects.summary: in, on, under, next to, opposite, behind…

aspects.title: some / any
aspects.summary: some en afirmativas, any en negativas y preguntas.

aspects.title: how much / how many
aspects.summary: how many con contables, how much con incontables y precios.

aspects.title: En la tienda
aspects.summary: Pedir un talle, preguntar el precio y comprar.

aspects.title: Dar direcciones
aspects.summary: Seguí derecho, doblá a la izquierda, está en la esquina…

aspects.title: Casa y muebles
aspects.summary: Las habitaciones y los objetos de la casa.

atoms.text: You have got a beautiful office, Mary. There's a coffee machine on that table, a plant next to the window, and a lamp behind the armchair.
atoms.gloss: Tenés una oficina hermosa, Mary. Hay una cafetera en esa mesa, una planta al lado de la ventana y una lámpara detrás del sillón.
atoms.speaker: colleague
atoms.ipa: juː hæv ɡɑːt ɐ bjˈuːɾifəl ˈɑːfɪs mˈɛɹi ðɛɹz ɐ kˈɔfi məʃˈiːn ˌɔn ðæt tˈeɪbəl ɐ plˈænt nˈɛkst tə ðə wˈɪndoʊ ænd ɐ lˈæmp bᵻhˌaɪnd ðɪ ˈɑːɹmtʃɛɹ

atoms.text: Thank you! We can see the Museum opposite our building.
atoms.gloss: ¡Gracias! Podemos ver el museo enfrente de nuestro edificio.
atoms.speaker: mary
atoms.ipa: θˈæŋk juː wiː kæn sˈiː ðə mjuːzˈiəm ˈɑːpəzˌɪt ˌaʊɚ bˈɪldɪŋ

atoms.text: Would you like to go to the Museum?
atoms.speaker: colleague
replies: Yes. What time does it open?
atoms.ipa: wʊd juː lˈaɪk tə ɡˌoʊ tə ðə mjuːzˈiəm

atoms.text: What time does it open?
atoms.speaker: mary
replies: It opens at eleven and closes at ten.
atoms.ipa: wˌʌt tˈaɪm dˈʌz ɪɾ ˈoʊpən

atoms.text: It opens at eleven and closes at ten.
atoms.gloss: Abre a las once y cierra a las diez.
atoms.speaker: colleague
atoms.ipa: ɪɾ ˈoʊpənz æɾ ᵻlˈɛvən ænd klˈoʊzᵻz æt tˈɛn

atoms.text: We can go there after work.
atoms.speaker: mary
atoms.ipa: wiː kæn ɡˌoʊ ðɛɹ ˈæftɚ wˈɜːk

atoms.text: There's a cafeteria next to the Museum.
atoms.gloss: Hay una cafetería al lado del museo.
atoms.speaker: mary
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹz ɐ kˌæfɪtˈɪɹiə nˈɛkst tə ðə mjuːzˈiəm

atoms.text: Are there buses late at night?
atoms.speaker: colleague
atoms.ipa: ɑːɹ ðɛɹ bˈʌsᵻz lˈeɪt æt nˈaɪt

atoms.title: La oficina y el museo
atoms.situation: El colega admira la oficina de Mary y coordinan ir al museo de enfrente.
variants: There is a plant next to the window.
Would you like to go to the museum?

atoms.text: Hi. Can I help you?
atoms.speaker: shopkeeper
atoms.ipa: hˈaɪ kæn aɪ hˈɛlp juː

atoms.text: Have you got this sweater in small?
atoms.speaker: student-m
atoms.ipa: hæv juː ɡɑːt ðɪs swˈɛɾɚɹ ɪn smˈɔːl

atoms.text: Just a moment. Here you are.
atoms.speaker: shopkeeper
atoms.ipa: dʒˈʌst ɐ mˈoʊmənt hˈɪɹ juː ɑːɹ

atoms.text: Oh no. It's too small.
atoms.speaker: student-m
atoms.ipa: ˈoʊ nˈoʊ ɪts tˈuː smˈɔːl

atoms.text: We've got it in medium.
atoms.speaker: shopkeeper
atoms.ipa: wiːv ɡɑːt ɪɾ ɪn mˈiːdiəm

atoms.text: Great. How much is it?
atoms.speaker: student-m
atoms.ipa: ɡɹˈeɪt hˌaʊ mˈʌtʃ ɪz ɪt

atoms.text: It's thirty dollars.
atoms.speaker: shopkeeper
atoms.ipa: ɪts θˈɜːɾi dˈɑːlɚz

atoms.text: Great. I'll take it.
atoms.speaker: student-m
atoms.ipa: ɡɹˈeɪt aɪl tˈeɪk ɪt

atoms.title: En la tienda
atoms.situation: Un cliente busca un suéter en su talle, pregunta el precio y lo compra.

atoms.text: There is a sofa in the living room.
atoms.gloss: Hay un sillón en el living.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹ ɪz ɐ sˈoʊfə ɪnðə lˈɪvɪŋ ɹˈuːm

atoms.text: There aren't students in the classroom.
atoms.gloss: No hay estudiantes en el aula.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹ ˌɑːɹnt stˈuːdənts ɪnðə klˈæsɹuːm

atoms.text: Is there a swimming pool in the hotel?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɪz ðɛɹ ɐ swˈɪmɪŋ pˈuːl ɪnðə hoʊtˈɛl

atoms.text: What is there on the top floor?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: wˌʌt ɪz ðɛɹ ɔnðə tˈɑːp flˈoːɹ

atoms.text: You can look at paintings in an art gallery.

atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: juː kæn lˈʊk æt pˈeɪntɪŋz ɪn ɐn ˈɑːɹt ɡˈælɚɹi

atoms.text: Can I have a cup of coffee?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: kæn aɪ hæv ɐ kˈʌp ʌv kˈɔfi

atoms.text: I can't swim.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪ kˈænt swˈɪm

atoms.text: Can you play the guitar?

atoms.ipa: kæn juː plˈeɪ ðə ɡɪtˈɑːɹ

atoms.text: The book is on the table.
atoms.gloss: El libro está sobre la mesa.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə bˈʊk ɪz ɔnðə tˈeɪbəl

atoms.text: The cat is under the chair.
atoms.gloss: El gato está debajo de la silla.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə kˈæt ɪz ˌʌndɚ ðə tʃˈɛɹ

atoms.text: The restaurant is next to the library.
atoms.gloss: El restaurante está al lado de la biblioteca.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə ɹˈɛstɹɑːnt ɪz nˈɛkst tə ðə lˈaɪbɹɛɹi

atoms.text: The park is opposite the museum.
atoms.gloss: El parque está enfrente del museo.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə pˈɑːɹk ɪz ˈɑːpəzˌɪt ðə mjuːzˈiəm

atoms.gloss: La habitación 435 está en el tercer piso.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɹˈuːm fˈoːɹhˈʌndɹɪd θˈɜːɾi fˈaɪv ɪz ɔnðə θˈɜːd flˈoːɹ

atoms.text: Go straight on until you get to the roundabout.
atoms.gloss: Seguí derecho hasta llegar a la rotonda.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɡˌoʊ stɹˈeɪt ˌɔn ʌntˈɪl juː ɡɛt tə ðə ɹˈaʊndɐbˌaʊt

atoms.text: Turn left at the traffic light.
atoms.gloss: Doblá a la izquierda en el semáforo.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: tˈɜːn lˈɛft æt ðə tɹˈæfɪk lˈaɪt

atoms.prompt: Excuse me, how do I get to the museum?
replies: Go straight on and turn left.
atoms.speaker: student

atoms.speaker: narrator

atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹ ɪz sˌʌm mˈɪlk ɪnðə fɹˈɪdʒ

atoms.gloss: No hay sillas en la cocina.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹ ˌɑːɹnt ˌɛni tʃˈɛɹz ɪnðə kˈɪtʃən

atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: hæv juː ɡɑːt ˌɛni bɹˈʌðɚz

atoms.text: Go over the bridge and turn right.
atoms.gloss: Cruzá el puente y doblá a la derecha.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɡˌoʊ ˌoʊvɚ ðə bɹˈɪdʒ ænd tˈɜːn ɹˈaɪt

atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ɪts bᵻtwˌiːn ðə kˈɑːɹ pˈɑːɹk ænd ðə ʃˈɑːpɪŋ sˈɛntɚ

atoms.text: Take the first street on the left.

atoms.ipa: tˈeɪk ðə fˈɜːst stɹˈiːt ɔnðə lˈɛft

atoms.speaker: narrator

atoms.text: There are books on the shelves.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹˌɑːɹ bˈʊks ɔnðə ʃˈɛlvz

atoms.gloss: La puerta está al lado de la ventana.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə dˈoːɹ ɪz nˈɛkst tə ðə wˈɪndoʊ

atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə kˈʌps ɑːɹ ɪnðə kˈʌbəd

atoms.gloss: Hay una alfombra en el living.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðɛɹ ɪz ɐ kˈɑːɹpɪt ɪnðə lˈɪvɪŋ ɹˈuːm

atoms.prompt: Describe a place (a building, a cultural centre). Say what there is and what you can do there.
atoms.modelAnswer: The CCK is the biggest Cultural Centre in Latin America. It is in Buenos Aires. There are five auditoriums, forty rooms and two terraces. There are also coffee shops and restaurants. Its design is French. There is a beautiful statue opposite the building. You can listen to music, have a cup of coffee, see paintings, or just relax there. All events are free.
modelVariants: The museum is in the centre of the city. There are three floors and many rooms. There is a café on the ground floor. You can see paintings and sculptures. There is a shop next to the entrance. It opens at ten and it is free on Sundays.

The shopping centre is near my house. There are many shops and two restaurants. There is a big car park under the building. You can buy clothes, eat something, or watch a movie. There is a pharmacy on the first floor.

rubric.text: Usa can para lo que se puede hacer
Describe el lugar con varios detalles

atoms.speaker: narrator

atoms.prompt: How many rooms are there in your flat?
atoms.speaker: student

atoms.prompt: Complete with there is or there are.

items.stem: ___ six chairs in the dining room.
___ a bus station next to the restaurant?

atoms.prompt: Complete the questions with how much or how many.

items.stem: ___ tables are there in your flat?
___ cups of coffee do you want?
___ schools are there in this town?
Are there ___ beds in this room?
There are ___ nice jackets over there.

atoms.prompt: Write a request with can for each situation.

items.stem: You want a student's telephone number.

accept: Can you give me your telephone number?
Can I have your telephone number?

items.stem: You want to know the date of the conference.

accept: Can you tell me the date of the conference?

items.stem: You want the book that he has got.

accept: Can you give me your book?

items.stem: You call room service for some coffee.

source.note: La narrativa NO está en el material (link ausente). Escrita con léxico de la U4, consistente con la grilla: 4 sillas en el living, sí hay heladera, 3 dormitorios.

atoms.narrative: Welcome to my house. This is the living room. There are four chairs and a big sofa. There is a television on the wall. Next to the living room there is the kitchen. In the kitchen there is a fridge and a washing machine. There are three bedrooms upstairs. There is a bathroom next to my bedroom. In the garden there are some flowers and a small table. I love my house.
atoms.speaker: narrator

questions.q: How many chairs are there in the living room?
Is there a fridge in the kitchen?

source.section: Actividad de cierre — Composition My Life Chapter 4

atoms.prompt: COMPOSITION: MY LIFE: CHAPTER 4. Describe the place where you live. Mention your house and your town or city.
atoms.modelAnswer: I live in a small apartment in the city. There is a living room, a kitchen and two bedrooms. There is a big window in the living room and there is a balcony. My town is nice. There is a square near my house and there is a pharmacy next to it. There are many shops and restaurants. You can walk everywhere. I love the place where I live.
modelVariants: I live in a big house with a garden. There are three bedrooms and two bathrooms. There is a garage next to the kitchen. My city is very big. There is a river and there are many parks. You can go running or ride a bike there.

rubric.text: Menciona la casa y la ciudad
Describe el lugar con varios detalles

atoms.speaker: examiner
